Good evening, and welcome to the Lucerne. Do you have a reservation with us tonight?
Good evening. Yes, I have a reservation under the name Ito. I've just landed, and I've already completed the online check-in.
Perfect. Could I have your confirmation number and your passport, please?
Here you are. The confirmation number is L-M-I, four seven two, N-Y.
Thank you. I do have one small problem, though. Housekeeping hasn't finished your room yet. It won't be ready for about forty minutes.
I understand. Could I leave my bags at the front desk and wait in the lobby? And is there anywhere near here to eat at this hour?
Of course. Our concierge has already put together a list for you, and there's an Italian place two blocks down on Amsterdam that's open till midnight. Once your key is ready, you're good to go.
Sorry, I didn't catch the name of the street. Could you spell it for me? And let me check I understood: you'll call me in the lobby when the room is ready, right?
Excuse me, I have a reservation under the name Ito.
I've just landed, and I haven't checked in yet.
I've already completed the online check-in. Here is my confirmation number.
Could you tell me where the baggage claim is?
My flight landed at nine, and I took a cab straight to the hotel.

Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to New York. We have just landed at John F. Kennedy International Airport, Terminal Four, and the local time is nine fifteen in the evening. Please remain seated with your seat belt fastened until the aircraft has come to a complete stop. If you have a connecting flight tonight, the transfer desk is on the upper level, and an agent will help you find your gate. If New York is your final destination, follow the signs to passport control, and then to baggage claim. Your checked bags will arrive on belt six. Please do not leave your carry-on in the overhead bin. On behalf of the crew, thank you for flying with us, and enjoy your stay in the city.

Hi, this is a message from the front desk. I am calling about your reservation for tonight. I have good news and one small problem. The good news first. We have already received your confirmation number, so your check-in is quick, and the concierge has already booked a table for you tomorrow at the Italian restaurant on the corner. Now the small problem. Your room is on the eighth floor, and the cleaning team has not finished it yet, so it will not be ready until three o'clock. If you arrive before that, please leave your bags with us and have a coffee in the lobby. Everything else is good to go. Call the front desk if you need anything at all. See you soon.
